en: I wish you would bear with me in a little foolishness. Do bear with me!
zh: 請你們容忍我這一點點的愚昧，其實你們本來就是容忍我的。

en: I feel a divine jealousy for you, for I betrothed you to one husband, to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
zh: 我以 神的熱愛愛你們，因為我把你們獻給基督，好像把貞潔的童女許配給一個丈夫。

en: But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning, your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
zh: 我只怕你們的心受到引誘，失去對基督的單純和貞潔，好像蛇用詭計騙了夏娃一樣。

en: For if someone comes and proclaims another Jesus than the one we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or if you accept a different gospel from the one you accepted, you put up with it readily enough.
zh: 如果有人來傳講另一位耶穌，不是我們傳過的，或者你們接受另一個不同的靈，不是你們領受過的，又或者接受另一個不同的福音，不是你們接受過的，你們倒可以容忍得下！

en: I consider that I am not in the least inferior to these super-apostles.
zh: 但我認為自己沒有一點比不上那些“超等使徒”；

en: Even if I am unskilled in speaking, I am not so in knowledge; indeed, in every way we have made this plain to you in all things.
zh: 雖然我不善於辭令，卻是有學問的，我們在各方面，已經向你們清楚顯明了。

en: Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I preached God's gospel to you free of charge?
zh: 我貶低自己，使你們高升，把 神的福音白白地傳給你們，我這樣是犯罪嗎？

en: I robbed other churches by accepting support from them in order to serve you.
zh: 我為了服事你們，就接受了別的教會的供應，也可以說是剝削了他們。

en: And when I was with you and was in need, I did not burden anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia supplied my need. So I refrained and will refrain from burdening you in any way.
zh: 我在你們那裡有缺乏的時候，並沒有連累任何人，因為從馬其頓來的弟兄們，補足了我的缺乏。我在各方面都不讓自己成為你們的重擔，將來也是一樣。

en: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boasting of mine will not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.
zh: 我有基督的真理在我裡面，在亞該亞一帶沒有人能阻止我這樣誇口。

en: And why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
zh: 為甚麼呢？是我不愛你們嗎？ 神是知道的。

en: And what I do I will continue to do, in order to undermine the claim of those who would like to claim that in their boasted mission they work on the same terms as we do.
zh: 我現在所作的，將來還要作，為了要斷絕那些投機分子的機會，不讓他們在所誇的事上，被人認為是跟我們一樣的。

en: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workmen, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
zh: 這樣的人是假使徒，是詭詐的工人，裝成基督的使徒。

en: And no wonder, for even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
zh: 這並不希奇，因為撒但自己也裝作光明的天使，

en: So it is no surprise if his servants, also, disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will correspond to their deeds.
zh: 所以，撒但的僕役裝成公義的僕役，也不必大驚小怪。他們的結局必按他們所作的而定。

en: I repeat, let no one think me foolish. But even if you do, accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.
zh: 我再說，誰也不要以為我是愚昧的；就算這樣，也要接納我這個愚昧的人，使我可以稍微地誇口。

en: What I am saying with this boastful confidence, I say not with the Lord's authority but as a fool.
zh: 我這樣滿有自信地誇口，不是照著主所吩咐的，而是好像愚昧人在說話。

en: Since many boast according to the flesh, I too will boast.
zh: 既然有許多人按著世俗的標準誇口，我也要誇口。

en: For you gladly bear with fools, being wise yourselves!
zh: 你們那麼精明，竟然樂意容忍愚昧的人。

en: For you bear it if someone makes slaves of you, or devours you, or takes advantage of you, or puts on airs, or strikes you in the face.
zh: 如果有人奴役你們，侵吞、榨取你們，向你們趾高氣揚，打你們的臉，你們就容忍吧！

en: To my shame, I must say, we were too weak for that! But whatever anyone else dares to boast of- I am speaking as a fool- I also dare to boast of that.
zh: 說來慚愧，我們太軟弱了。說句愚昧的話，如果有人在甚麼事上是勇敢的，我也是勇敢的。

en: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they offspring of Abraham? So am I.
zh: 他們是希伯來人嗎？我也是。他們是以色列人嗎？我也是。他們是亞伯拉罕的後裔嗎？我也是。

en: Are they servants of Christ? I am a better one- I am talking like a madman- with far greater labors, far more imprisonments, with countless beatings, and often near death.
zh: 他們是基督的僕人嗎？說句狂話，我更是。我受更多的勞苦，更多的坐監，受了過量的鞭打，常常有生命的危險。

en: Five times I received at the hands of the Jews the forty lashes less one.
zh: 我被猶太人打過五次，每次四十下減去一下，

en: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I was shipwrecked; a night and a day I was adrift at sea;
zh: 被棍打過三次，被石頭打過一次，三次遇著船壞，在深海裡飄了一晝一夜；

en: on frequent journeys, in danger from rivers, danger from robbers, danger from my own people, danger from Gentiles, danger in the city, danger in the wilderness, danger at sea, danger from false brothers;
zh: 多次行遠路，遇著江河的危險、強盜的危險、同族的危險、外族的危險、城中的危險、曠野的危險、海上的危險、假弟兄的危險；

en: in toil and hardship, through many a sleepless night, in hunger and thirst, often without food, in cold and exposure.
zh: 勞碌辛苦，多次不得睡覺，又飢又渴，多次缺糧，赤身挨冷。

en: And, apart from other things, there is the daily pressure on me of my anxiety for all the churches.
zh: 除了這些外面的事，還有為各教會掛心的事，天天壓在我的身上。

en: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to fall, and I am not indignant?
zh: 有誰軟弱，我不軟弱呢？有誰陷在罪裡，我不焦急呢？

en: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.
zh: 如果必須誇口，我就誇自己的弱點。

en: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, he who is blessed forever, knows that I am not lying.
zh: 主耶穌的父 神是應當永遠受稱頌的，他知道我沒有說謊。

en: At Damascus, the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to seize me,
zh: 在大馬士革，亞里達王手下的總督把守著大馬士革城，要拘捕我，

en: but I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall and escaped his hands.
zh: 我就被人放在一個大籃子裡，從城牆的窗戶縋下來，逃脫了他的手。
